steps.title: Understand the problem: temperature increase conversion
steps.content:
The function F(x) converts Kelvin to Fahrenheit. The question asks about the Fahrenheit increase corresponding to a 13.30-Kelvin increase.

Since temperature increases are being compared, constant terms in the conversion formula (+32, -273.15) cancel out.

Only the scaling factor $\frac{9}{5}$ (equivalent to $1.8$) between Kelvin/Celsius and Fahrenheit matters.

Compute: $13.30 \times \frac{9}{5}$

steps.title: Direct multiplication for temperature difference
steps.content:
Multiply the Kelvin increase by the Fahrenheit scale factor:
$$13.30 \times \frac{9}{5} = 13.30 \times 1.8 = 23.94$$
This matches the linear relationship between temperature differences, bypassing full function evaluation. That matches choice A — A is correct.
